content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814


July 5, 1977


Francis P. Pusateri
Manager
California Potato Research
Advisory Board
P.O. Box 2342
Bakersfield, California 93303

Dear Mr. Pusateri:

Thank you for your inquiry of June 14, 1977, regarding the
potential filing obligations of agricultural marketing order
board members.

As presently construed, the proposed Code of the Department
of Food and Agriculture covering members of agricultural marketing
orders, as revised by the staff of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, would require members to disclose in the following
manner:

1. All investments in business entities or income
from sources of the type which contract with the order
for the delivery of advertising services.

2. Investments in each business entity, income from
each source, and any real property over which the
member's board has jurisdiction.

The second category may include for some boards both handlers
and producers. This appears to be the large problem area because
the Act requires, in Government Code §87207, that income received
by the business entity in which the individual member has an
interest may be disclosable in addition to the business entity
itself. The code will require that the customers of the member's
business entity be disclosed where:

1. The member has at least a 10% interest in a
business entity, including a farm, and

2. The member's pro rata share of the gross fees
paid by the customer to the business entity is $1,000
or more, and

Francis P. Pusateri
July 1, 1977
page two


     3.  The customer is regulated by or subject to the
jurisdiction of the member's board or order.

     The form upon which it will be necessary for the member to
disclose this information is enclosed at this Division's model
form marked with a paper clip.

     If you request further information with respect to this
matter, please feel free to call me at (916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mfa
Enclosure

California Potato Research Advisory Board
P. O. Box 2342 — Bakersfield, Calif. 93303 — Telephone (805) 834-6685

June 14, 1977                                                File 191-J


Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

This is in reference to your memorandum of June 9, 1977 and to
certain discussions that took place during the course of the
Commission's meeting on June 8 relating to the Department of
Food and Agriculture's proposed Conflict of Interest Code.

In order that members of this Board may clearly prepare appro-
priate testimony for presentation at the August 17, 1977 hearing,
please forward the undersigned -- in non-legal layman's language --
the proposed Code's application to marketing order advisory board
members, including clarification of "income of board members
derived from commercial transactions involving the purchase or
sale of a commodity which is subject of the board program", and
format of the reporting document which advisory board members
would be required to complete.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Francis P. Pusateri
Manager

FPPb

cc: Claude Aunger, CPRAB Chairman


administering research and development programs under authority of the director of food and agriculture, state of california

California Potato Research Advisory Board
P. O. Box 2342 — Bakersfield, Calif. 93303 — Telephone (805) 834-6685

June 14, 1977                                                File 191-J


Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

This is in reference to your memorandum of June 9, 1977 and to
certain discussions that took place during the course of the
Commission's meeting on June 8 relating to the Department of
Food and Agriculture's proposed Conflict of Interest Code.

In order that members of this Board may clearly prepare appro-
priate testimony for presentation at the August 17, 1977 hearing,
please forward the undersigned -- in non-legal layman's language --
the proposed Code's application to marketing order advisory board
members, including clarification of "income of board members
derived from commercial transactions involving the purchase or
sale of a commodity which is subject of the board program", and
format of the reporting document which advisory board members
would be required to complete.

Very truly yours,

Francis P. Pusateri
Manager

FPPb

cc: Claude Aunger, CPRAB Chairman


administering research and development programs under authority of the director of food and agriculture, state of california

sections.question: What are the conflict of interest obligations of agricultural marketing order board members, and specifically how should they disclose income derived from commercial transactions involving the purchase or sale of a commodity which is subject of the board program?
sections.conclusion: Members would be required to disclose: (1) All investments in business entities from certain sources; (2) Customer relationships meeting three criteria: the member has at least a 10% business interest in a farm or similar entity, the customer's business depends on delivery to or purchases from the business entity, and the customer is regulated by or subject to the jurisdiction of the member's board or order.
sections.question_synthetic: The inquiry seeks clarification on conflict of interest disclosure requirements for members of agricultural marketing order advisory boards, particularly regarding income from commercial transactions involving commodities subject to the board's jurisdiction.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that agricultural marketing order board members must disclose investments in business entities and customer relationships that meet specific threshold requirements, including a 10% ownership interest and dependence on the board-regulated entity.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant OCR errors and formatting issues. The incoming request letter is included (dated June 14, 1977 from Francis P. Pusateri), followed by the FPPC response (dated July 5, 1977 from Delbert L. Spurlock Jr.). Some sections of the conclusion are partially illegible due to document quality, but the main requirements are discernible. Key three-part test for disclosure is identified but some specific language may be slightly corrupted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the conflict of interest obligations of agricultural marketing order board members, and specifically how should they disclose income derived from commercial transactions involving the purchase or sale of a commodity which is subject of the board program?

CONCLUSION: Members would be required to disclose: (1) All investments in business entities from certain sources; (2) Customer relationships meeting three criteria: the member has at least a 10% business interest in a farm or similar entity, the customer's business depends on delivery to or purchases from the business entity, and the customer is regulated by or subject to the jurisdiction of the member's board or order.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to an inquiry from the California Potato Research Advisory Board regarding disclosure obligations under a proposed conflict of interest code for agricultural marketing order board members. The FPPC provides specific disclosure requirements and criteria for determining which business relationships and investments must be reported.
